summary: Scorching Bertha and tight jams; strong second‑set flow
blurb: The first set builds with bright renditions of Sugaree and Ramble On Rose, then the band lifts into an energetic second set highlighted by a fiery Bertha, powerful Playing In The Band, and a soaring Dear Mr. Fantasy. Jerry’s guitar stays on fire throughout while Phil and the dual drummers keep the momentum high.
review:
On August 18 1987 at Tempe’s Compton Terrace Amphitheatre the Dead delivered a summer‑night set that still feels fresh. The first set opened with solid staples—Sugaree, Ramble On Rose and My Brother Esau—setting a bright tempo that carried into a blistering Bertha. Jerry Garcia was “ON” all night, his lead on Ramble On Rose and Crazy Fingers described as swing‑errific, while Brent’s keyboards went crazy on Dear Mr. Fantasy, matching the band’s intensity. The second set was a showcase of the group’s chemistry: Phil Lesh shone on Greatest Story Ever Told and Playing In The Band, and the twin drummers (Mickey & Billy) delivered an amazing drum‑Space segment that propelled the music into soaring territory before closing with a party‑closing Turn On Your Lovelight. While a few recordings note occasional uneven moments, the consensus across sources is that this show was consistently strong, making it one of the standout summer ’87 performances.

For listeners seeking the best listening experience, the patched FM‑matrix composite (gd1987-08-18.145599.nak300.in-house.fm.composite.hessberg.miller.clugston.flac1648) captures the energy with good overall fidelity despite some frequency quirks, and an excellent audience recording (gd1987-08-18.142155.fob.schoeps.cmc3.mk2.oade.jacobs.miller.clugston.flac1644) offers matrix‑like clarity for those who prefer crowd ambience.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four of six analyses describe the performance as legendary or consistently strong, praising Bertha, Playing In The Band and the dual drummers; two mixed reviews note some uneven moments but do not detract from overall positivity.

best_recording.reason: FM‑matrix composite offers the most complete capture with good overall fidelity, preserving the band’s energy despite minor frequency quirks.

key_highlights: Scorching Bertha with Jerry on fire throughout.
Tight jams and high‑energy second set, especially Dear Mr. Fantasy and Throwing Stones.
Phil Lesh shines on Greatest Story Ever Told and Playing In The Band; dual drummers amaze.
FM matrix composite provides solid sound despite frequency quirks; audience recording offers excellent clarity.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar stays on fire all night, delivering swing‑errific leads on Ramble On Rose and Crazy Fingers; raw tone noted in some audience tapes but overall stellar.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh shines on Greatest Story Ever Told and Playing In The Band, providing a strong bass foundation and energetic interplay with the drummers.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s rhythm work drives the band forward on My Brother Esau and Throwing Stones, adding overdrive and tight comping.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland’s keyboards go crazy on Dear Mr. Fantasy, matching the band's intensity; also praised for virile playing on Wharf Rat.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart delivers amazing drum work throughout, especially in the dynamic Drums/Space segment.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann pairs with Hart to create a powerful dual‑drum foundation that propels the second set.
